title: Unified companion transport TCP/BLE/USB in one firmware, with runtime switching and no reflashing

messages.author: Just Stuff™
messages.text: Companion WiFi/BLE/USB is now unified in one Heltec v3 firmware, Testing on V2 (Feel Free To Fork and Open a PR against this branch to add more supported boards) you can switch transport modes at runtime with no reflashing. You can also change WiFi settings live ( wifi.mode , wifi.ssid , wifi.pwd , wifi.ap.ssid , wifi.ap.pwd ) and they persist across reboot. ). https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1968 (edited)
messages.content_html: Companion WiFi/BLE/USB is now unified in one Heltec v3 firmware, Testing on V2 (Feel Free To Fork and Open a PR against this branch to add more supported boards) you can switch transport modes at runtime with no reflashing. You can also change WiFi settings live (wifi.mode, wifi.ssid, wifi.pwd, wifi.ap.ssid, wifi.ap.pwd) and they persist across reboot. ).https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1968 (edited)
embeds_html:
(Meshcore): runtime companion transport switching (BLE <-> WiFi/TCP...
Feel Free To Fork and add additional board support And Submit PRs To this Branch, Lets get upstream to accept........ This PR adds a unified ESP32 companion transport layer for Heltec v3 that allow...

messages.author: Just Stuff™
messages.text: 3.2 meshcli interactive mode (recommended for manual testing) Start interactive mode: meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 -b 115200 Example interactive session: KD3CGK|* set wifi.mode client Var wifi.mode set to client KD3CGK|* get wifi.mode client KD3CGK|* set transport ble Var transport set to ble KD3CGK|* get transport ble KD3CGK|* get wifi.ssid SampleSSID KD3CGK|* get wifi.ap.ssid MeshCore-SampleAP KD3CGK|* get wifi.pwd SamplePwd123 KD3CGK|* get wifi.ap.pwd SampleAPPwd123
messages.content_html:
3.2 meshcli interactive mode (recommended for manual testing)

Start interactive mode: meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 -b 115200 Example interactive session: KD3CGK|* set wifi.mode client Var wifi.mode set to client KD3CGK|* get wifi.mode client KD3CGK|* set transport ble Var transport set to ble KD3CGK|* get transport ble KD3CGK|* get wifi.ssid SampleSSID KD3CGK|* get wifi.ap.ssid MeshCore-SampleAP KD3CGK|* get wifi.pwd SamplePwd123 KD3CGK|* get wifi.ap.pwd SampleAPPwd123

messages.author: Just Stuff™
Just Stuff™
messages.text: Build And Flash This Fork (Heltec V3) If you want to build and flash this fork directly from terminal: git clone https://github.com/just-stuff-tm/MeshCore.git cd MeshCore git checkout feat/heltec-v3-runtime-transport-dev Install PlatformIO Core (if not already installed): python3 -m pip install -U platformio Build firmware: ~/.platformio/penv/bin/pio run -e Heltec_v3_companion_radio_tcp_usb_ble Flash firmware (USB serial example): ~/.platformio/penv/bin/pio run -e Heltec_v3_companion_radio_tcp_usb_ble -t upload --upload-port /dev/ttyUSB0 Optional serial companion check: meshcli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 (edited)
messages.content_html:
Build And Flash This Fork (Heltec V3)

If you want to build and flash this fork directly from terminal: git clone https://github.com/just-stuff-tm/MeshCore.git cd MeshCore git checkout feat/heltec-v3-runtime-transport-dev Install PlatformIO Core (if not already installed): python3 -m pip install -U platformio Build firmware: ~/.platformio/penv/bin/pio run -e Heltec_v3_companion_radio_tcp_usb_ble Flash firmware (USB serial example): ~/.platformio/penv/bin/pio run -e Heltec_v3_companion_radio_tcp_usb_ble -t upload --upload-port /dev/ttyUSB0 Optional serial companion check: meshcli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 (edited)

messages.author: Just Stuff™
messages.text: Set Commands In meshcli set transport ble set wifi.mode ap set wifi.ap.ssid MeshCore-SampleAP set wifi.ap.pwd SampleAPPwd123 set wifi.mode client set wifi.ssid SampleSSID set wifi.pwd SamplePwd123 The Next 2 enable the exact same thing set transport tcp set transport wifi set tcp.port 5000 --- Get Commands get transport get wifi.mode get wifi.ap.ssid get wifi.ap.pwd get wifi.ssid get wifi.pwd get tcp.port (edited)
messages.content_html:
Set Commands In meshcli

set transport ble set wifi.mode ap set wifi.ap.ssid MeshCore-SampleAP set wifi.ap.pwd SampleAPPwd123 set wifi.mode client set wifi.ssid SampleSSID set wifi.pwd SamplePwd123 The Next 2 enable the exact same thing set transport tcp set transport wifi set tcp.port 5000 ---

Get Commands

get transport get wifi.mode get wifi.ap.ssid get wifi.ap.pwd get wifi.ssid get wifi.pwd get tcp.port (edited)

messages.author: Just Stuff™
messages.text: “Thanks, that’s helpful. I don’t have a Heltec V2 locally, so I fixed the issues I could verify from the code and from builds, but I still need your retest on actual V2 hardware. I’ve already adjusted the V2 transport build limits to more usable values: MAX_CONTACTS=70 MAX_GROUP_CHANNELS=8 OFFLINE_QUEUE_SIZE=200 I also fixed the false Connected state in the all-transports UI. That was a real bug in the transport connection reporting, not expected behavior. For the WiFi modes: client is the same as the old dedicated WiFi build, where the node joins an existing WiFi network. ap is the new hotspot mode, where the node creates its own WiFi network for direct connection. For transport switching, USB should still be usable in the all-transports build even when BLE is the selected wireless mode. set transport ble/tcp/wifi only changes the wireless side. A normal serial terminal won’t work for the framed companion protocol unless you use meshcli I fixed the code paths for the reported issues, but since I don’t have a V2 on hand, I’d appreciate a retest on your side for: 1. false Connected state 2. long-press/triple-click transport switching 3. usable contact/channel/offline queue sizing 4. USB access via meshcli ” I'm still testing these changes on v3 (edited)
messages.content_html: “Thanks, that’s helpful. I don’t have a Heltec V2 locally, so I fixed the issues I could verify from the code and from builds, but I still need your retest on actual V2 hardware. I’ve already adjusted the V2 transport build limits to more usable values: MAX_CONTACTS=70 MAX_GROUP_CHANNELS=8 OFFLINE_QUEUE_SIZE=200 I also fixed the false Connected state in the all-transports UI. That was a real bug in the transport connection reporting, not expected behavior. For the WiFi modes: client is the same as the old dedicated WiFi build, where the node joins an existing WiFi network. ap is the new hotspot mode, where the node creates its own WiFi network for direct connection. For transport switching, USB should still be usable in the all-transports build even when BLE is the selected wireless mode. set transport ble/tcp/wifi only changes the wireless side. A normal serial terminal won’t work for the framed companion protocol unless you use meshcli I fixed the code paths for the reported issues, but since I don’t have a V2 on hand, I’d appreciate a retest on your side for: 1. false Connected state 2. long-press/triple-click transport switching 3. usable contact/channel/offline queue sizing 4. USB access via meshcli” I'm still testing these changes on v3 (edited)

messages.author: Just Stuff™
messages.text: Also to clarify a couple of points from your notes: Double press is not intended to switch transports. On the single-button UI the intended mapping is: single click = next page double click = previous page long press on BLE page = switch wireless transport to WiFi/TCP long press on WiFi page = toggle AP/client triple click on WiFi page = switch wireless transport back to BLE USB is not a separate mode in this build. USB should remain available as the wired companion interface all the time, while set transport ble/tcp/wifi only changes the wireless side. A normal serial terminal like CoolTerm will not talk to the runtime USB interface correctly, because the default USB interface is the framed companion protocol, not the raw text CLI. For normal USB testing you need meshcli or another client that speaks the companion protocol. The raw text CLI is only available through CLI Rescue during boot. So if you retest after the next build, the main extra thing I’m interested in is whether V2 is still unexpectedly rebooting on double-click, since that is not supposed to happen.
messages.content_html:
Also to clarify a couple of points from your notes:
  • Double press is not intended to switch transports. On the single-button UI the intended mapping is:
single click = next page double click = previous page long press on BLE page = switch wireless transport to WiFi/TCP long press on WiFi page = toggle AP/client triple click on WiFi page = switch wireless transport back to BLE
  • USB is not a separate mode in this build. USB should remain available as the wired companion interface all the time, while set transport ble/tcp/wifi only changes the wireless side.
  • A normal serial terminal like CoolTerm will not talk to the runtime USB interface correctly, because the default USB interface is the framed companion protocol, not the raw text CLI. For normal USB testing you need meshcli or another client that speaks the companion protocol. The raw text CLI is only available through CLI Rescue during boot.
So if you retest after the next build, the main extra thing I’m interested in is whether V2 is still unexpectedly rebooting on double-click, since that is not supposed to happen.
